@fileOperations.button.cancel.description: Button that closes a copy/move/delete/new-file/new-folder dialog without taking the action. Short control label.

@fileOperations.button.ok.description: Confirm button in the new-file and new-folder dialogs. Short control label; this is the affirmative OK, not a status word.

@fileOperations.delete.fromPath.description: Header line in the delete-confirm dialog showing the folder the items are deleted from. The path is abbreviated (home shown as ~).

@fileOperations.delete.noTrashWarningStr.description: Bold lead of a warning banner in the delete dialog, shown on volumes (FAT32, SMB, etc.) that have no trash. Followed by fileOperations.delete.noTrashWarningRest in the same sentence.

@fileOperations.delete.noTrashWarningRes.description: Second half of the no-trash warning banner, following fileOperations.delete.noTrashWarningStrong. Tells the user the delete is permanent on this volume.

@fileOperations.delete.toggleTrash.description: Label of the segmented-control option that moves items to the system trash (recoverable). Short control label, sits next to the Delete option.

@fileOperations.delete.toggleDelete.description: Label of the segmented-control option that permanently deletes items. Short control label, sits next to the Trash option.

@fileOperations.delete.overflowMore.description: Trailing line of the delete dialog's file list when more items are selected than the list shows. Keep the leading ellipsis '...'.

placeholders.countText: how many extra items, formatted for display (e.g. 1,234)
placeholders.count: the same number of extra items (drives the singular/plural form of the noun)

@fileOperations.delete.scanFile.description: Unit noun shown after the live file tally in the delete dialog's scan stats (e.g. '123 files'). The number is rendered separately; this is only the noun.

placeholders.count: the file count (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.delete.scanDir.description: Unit noun shown after the live directory tally in the delete dialog's scan stats (e.g. '4 dirs'). 'dir' is the abbreviation of directory/folder. The number is rendered separately.

placeholders.count: the directory count (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.delete.throughputFiles.description: Live scan throughput in the delete dialog: files processed per second. '/s' means per second.

placeholders.rateText: the files-per-second rate, formatted for display (e.g. 1,200)

@fileOperations.delete.confirmDeletePerm.description: Primary (destructive) confirm button in the delete dialog when permanent delete is selected. Control label.

@fileOperations.delete.confirmMoveToTras.description: Primary confirm button in the delete dialog when the trash option is selected. Control label.

@fileOperations.delete.titleCursorFile.description: Delete dialog title when deleting the single file the cursor is on (nothing multi-selected). 'under cursor' means the highlighted row.

@fileOperations.delete.titleCursorFolder.description: Delete dialog title when deleting the single folder the cursor is on (nothing multi-selected).

@fileOperations.delete.titleFallback.description: Delete dialog title used when there is nothing to count (empty selection). Just the verb.

@fileOperations.delete.titleSelected.description: Delete dialog title for a multi-item selection. {phrase} is the already-built count phrase (e.g. '3 selected files and 1 folder').

placeholders.phrase: the pre-rendered counts phrase, e.g. '3 selected files and 1 folder'

@fileOperations.delete.selectedFilesPart.description: The files part of the delete-dialog count phrase, e.g. '3 selected files'. Combined with the folders part by fileOperations.shared.andJoin.

placeholders.countText: how many files (plain number, not grouped)
placeholders.count: the same number of files (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.delete.foldersPart.description: The folders part of the delete-dialog count phrase, e.g. '1 folder' or '2 folders'. Combined with the files part by fileOperations.shared.andJoin.

placeholders.countText: how many folders (plain number, not grouped)
placeholders.count: the same number of folders (drives the singular/plural noun)

fileOperations.delete.symlinkNoticeSingl: This item is a symlink. Only the link will be deleted, not its target.

@fileOperations.delete.symlinkNoticeSing.description: Notice in the delete dialog when the only selected item is a symbolic link. Reassures that the link is removed, not the file it points to. 'symlink'/'target' are filesystem terms.

fileOperations.delete.symlinkNoticeMany: Your selection includes {countText} {count, plural, one {symlink} other {symlinks}}. Only the links themselves will be deleted, not their targets.

@fileOperations.delete.symlinkNoticeMany.description: Notice in the delete dialog when the selection contains one or more symbolic links among other items. Reassures that only the links are removed. 'symlink'/'target' are filesystem terms.

placeholders.countText: how many symlinks (plain number, not grouped)
placeholders.count: the same number of symlinks (drives the singular/plural noun)

fileOperations.mkfile.title: New file

@fileOperations.mkfile.title.description: Title of the dialog that creates a new empty file (Shift+F4).

@fileOperations.mkfile.createIn.description: Subtitle of the new-file dialog naming the destination folder. <dir> wraps the folder name, which is styled (bold). Keep the sentence; only the folder name is highlighted.

@fileOperations.mkfile.nameAria.description: Accessibility label for the new file's name text field (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.mkfile.placeholder.description: Placeholder text inside the new-file name field, showing an example filename. 'notes.txt' is an example file name; translate 'Example' but keep a plausible example filename.

fileOperations.mkdir.title: New folder

@fileOperations.mkdir.title.description: Title of the dialog that creates a new folder (F7).

@fileOperations.mkdir.createIn.description: Subtitle of the new-folder dialog naming the parent folder. <dir> wraps the folder name, which is styled (bold). Keep the sentence; only the folder name is highlighted.

@fileOperations.mkdir.nameAria.description: Accessibility label for the new folder's name text field (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.mkdir.placeholder.description: Placeholder text inside the new-folder name field, showing an example folder name. 'my-project' is an example; translate 'Example' but keep a plausible example folder name.

fileOperations.mkdir.timeoutMessage: Couldn''t confirm the folder was created. The volume may be slow, so the folder may still have been created.

@fileOperations.mkdir.timeoutMessage.description: Warning in the new-folder dialog when the create command did not confirm in time (slow volume). Reassures the folder may still exist. Conversational, not alarming.

@fileOperations.mkdir.timeoutRefresh.description: Button in the new-folder timeout warning that reloads the current folder's file list to check whether the folder appeared. Control label.

@fileOperations.mkdir.timeoutDismiss.description: Button in the new-folder timeout warning that closes the warning without refreshing. Control label.

@fileOperations.mkdir.aiSuggestionsAria.description: Accessibility label for the region listing AI-generated folder-name suggestions (screen-reader only). 'AI' is the common abbreviation.

@fileOperations.mkdir.aiSuggestionsHeade.description: Heading shown above the AI-generated folder-name suggestion chips in the new-folder dialog. 'AI' is the common abbreviation.

fileOperations.shared.conflictExistsFold: There is already a folder by this name in this folder.

@fileOperations.shared.conflictExistsFol.description: Inline validation error in the new-file and new-folder dialogs when the typed name matches an existing folder in the current directory.

fileOperations.shared.conflictExistsFile: There is already a file by this name in this folder.

@fileOperations.shared.conflictExistsFil.description: Inline validation error in the new-file and new-folder dialogs when the typed name matches an existing file in the current directory.

@fileOperations.shared.byteRate.description: Data-transfer speed shown next to a scan or copy/move bar, e.g. '1.2 MB/s'. <size> renders the formatted size (a widget, no inner text); '/s' means per second. Keep the per-second marker adjacent to the size.

@fileOperations.shared.andJoin.description: Joins two count parts into one phrase, e.g. '3 files' and '1 folder' become '3 files and 1 folder'. Used in delete and transfer dialog titles. Reorder the parts if your language needs it.

placeholders.a: the first part (the files count phrase)
placeholders.b: the second part (the folders count phrase)

@fileOperations.shared.actionLabel.description: Field label shown before the Copy/Move (copy dialog) or Trash/Delete (delete dialog) segmented control. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.shared.scanningTooltip.description: Tooltip and screen-reader label for the spinner shown while the dialog counts the selected items (files, folders, total size).

@fileOperations.shared.scanCompleteToolt.description: Tooltip and screen-reader label for the checkmark shown once the dialog has finished counting the selected items.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.toggleCop.description: Label of the segmented-control option that sets the operation to copy. Short control label, sits next to Move.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.toggleMov.description: Label of the segmented-control option that sets the operation to move. Short control label, sits next to Copy.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.routeLabe.description: Field label shown before the source-to-destination line (e.g. ''Downloads -> Home'') in the copy/move dialog. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.destVolum.description: Accessibility label for the dropdown that picks which volume (disk/device) the files go to (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.transferDialog.destPathA.description: Accessibility label for the text field holding the destination folder path (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.transferDialog.spaceInfo.description: Free-space line next to the destination-volume picker, e.g. '12 GB free of 500 GB'. Both values are already formatted sizes.

placeholders.total: total volume size, already formatted (e.g. 500 GB)

fileOperations.transferDialog.smbNativeN: This share uses the system connection. Cancellation may be delayed. Use "Connect directly" in the volume picker for faster transfers and reliable cancel.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.smbNative.description: Note shown when the destination is an SMB share mounted by the operating system rather than connected directly by Cmdr. Warns that cancelling a transfer may lag. Keep the quoted "Connect directly" matching that picker action's label.

fileOperations.transferDialog.targetWill: This folder doesn''t exist yet. Cmdr will create it during the copy.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.targetWil.description: Yellow inline warning beneath the destination path box in the Copy/Move dialog, shown when the typed destination folder doesn''t exist yet and the Copy action is selected. Reassures the user the folder will be made automatically. Active voice, friendly. Keep the product name 'Cmdr' as-is. There''s a separate '...Move' variant for the Move action.

fileOperations.transferDialog.targetWill: This folder doesn''t exist yet. Cmdr will create it during the move.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.targetWil.description: Yellow inline warning beneath the destination path box in the Copy/Move dialog, shown when the typed destination folder doesn''t exist yet and the Move action is selected. Reassures the user the folder will be made automatically. Active voice, friendly. Keep the product name 'Cmdr' as-is. There''s a separate '...Copy' variant for the Copy action.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.scanFile.description: Unit noun after the live file tally in the transfer dialog's scan stats (e.g. '123 files'). The number is rendered separately.

placeholders.count: the file count (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.transferDialog.scanDir.description: Unit noun after the live directory tally in the transfer dialog's scan stats (e.g. '4 dirs'). 'dir' abbreviates directory/folder. The number is rendered separately.

placeholders.count: the directory count (drives the singular/plural noun)

fileOperations.transferDialog.hardlinkNo: <written></written> will be written. The source is <ondisk></ondisk> on disk – the extra is hardlinked files, which can''t stay linked across drives.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.hardlinkN.description: Note explaining why the bytes a copy will write exceed the source's on-disk size: hardlinked files become separate copies on another drive. <written> renders the to-be-written size and <ondisk> renders the source's on-disk size, both as inline size widgets (no inner text to translate). 'hardlinked' is a filesystem term. The dash is an en dash.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.checkingC.description: Status shown while the transfer dialog scans the destination for name clashes before the copy/move. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

fileOperations.transferDialog.mergeInfoS: 1 folder will merge with an existing folder

@fileOperations.transferDialog.mergeInfo.description: Informational line in the transfer dialog when exactly one same-named folder exists at the destination and will be merged (not replaced). Neutral, not a warning.

fileOperations.transferDialog.mergeInfoM: {countText} folders will merge with existing folders

@fileOperations.transferDialog.mergeInfo.description: Informational line in the transfer dialog when several same-named folders exist at the destination and will be merged. Neutral, not a warning.

placeholders.countText: how many folders will merge, formatted for display (e.g. 1,234)

fileOperations.transferDialog.conflictsS: {countText} {count, plural, one {file already exists} other {files already exist}}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.conflicts.description: Summary in the transfer dialog of how many destination files clash with the ones being transferred. The whole verb phrase agrees with the count ('1 file already exists' / '3 files already exist').

placeholders.countText: how many clashing files, formatted for display (e.g. 1,234)
placeholders.count: the same number (drives the singular/plural verb phrase)

@fileOperations.transferDialog.policySki.description: Radio option: leave clashing files untouched. Wording is 'Skip' for a single clash, 'Skip all' when there are several. Short control label.

placeholders.count: number of clashing files (selects the single vs. 'all' wording)

@fileOperations.transferDialog.policyOve.description: Radio option: replace clashing files with the ones being transferred. 'Overwrite' for one clash, 'Overwrite all' for several. Short control label.

placeholders.count: number of clashing files (selects the single vs. 'all' wording)

fileOperations.transferDialog.policyOver: {count, plural, one {Overwrite if smaller} other {Overwrite all smaller}}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.policyOve.description: Radio option: replace a clashing file only when the existing one is smaller. 'Overwrite if smaller' for one clash, 'Overwrite all smaller' for several. Control label.

placeholders.count: number of clashing files (selects the single vs. 'all' wording)

fileOperations.transferDialog.policyOver: {count, plural, one {Overwrite if older} other {Overwrite all older}}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.policyOve.description: Radio option: replace a clashing file only when the existing one is older. 'Overwrite if older' for one clash, 'Overwrite all older' for several. Control label.

placeholders.count: number of clashing files (selects the single vs. 'all' wording)

fileOperations.transferDialog.policyStop: {count, plural, one {Ask later} other {Ask for each}}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.policySto.description: Radio option: decide per clash during the transfer instead of choosing now. 'Ask later' for one clash, 'Ask for each' for several. Control label.

placeholders.count: number of clashing files (selects the single vs. 'each' wording)

fileOperations.transferDialog.typeMismat: Some clashes mix a file and a folder by the same name. Overwriting will replace items of a different type, including the entire contents of a folder.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.typeMisma.description: Red warning in the transfer dialog when 'Overwrite all' is chosen and at least one clash pairs a file with a same-named folder. Stresses the destructive swap. Keep it firm.

fileOperations.transferDialog.titleVerbO: {verb, select, copy {Copy} move {Move} delete {Delete} other {Move to trash}}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.titleVerb.description: Transfer/delete dialog title verb alone, used when there is nothing to count. 'Move to trash' is the trash variant. Matches the operation chosen.

placeholders.verb: the operation: copy, move, delete, or trash

fileOperations.transferDialog.titleWithC: {verb, select, copy {Copy} move {Move} delete {Delete} other {Move to trash}} {phrase}

@fileOperations.transferDialog.titleWith.description: Transfer/delete dialog title combining the operation verb with a counts phrase, e.g. 'Copy 2 files and 3 folders'. {phrase} is already built.

placeholders.verb: the operation: copy, move, delete, or trash
placeholders.phrase: the pre-rendered counts phrase, e.g. '2 files and 3 folders'

@fileOperations.transferDialog.filesPart.description: The files part of a transfer-dialog title phrase, e.g. '2 files'. Combined with the folders part by fileOperations.shared.andJoin.

placeholders.countText: how many files (plain number, not grouped)
placeholders.count: the same number of files (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.transferDialog.foldersPa.description: The folders part of a transfer-dialog title phrase, e.g. '3 folders'. Combined with the files part by fileOperations.shared.andJoin.

placeholders.countText: how many folders (plain number, not grouped)
placeholders.count: the same number of folders (drives the singular/plural noun)

fileOperations.transferDialog.pathErrorS: Can''t {verb, select, copy {copy} other {move}} "{name}" into its own subfolder

@fileOperations.transferDialog.pathError.description: Validation error under the destination-path field when the chosen destination is inside the item being transferred. The verb matches the operation (copy/move). The name is the source folder/file name, kept in straight double quotes.

placeholders.name: the source folder or file name

@fileOperations.transferDialog.pathError.description: Validation error under the destination-path field when the destination is the item's current parent folder, so the transfer would be a no-op. The name is kept in straight double quotes.

placeholders.name: the source folder or file name

@fileOperations.transferDialog.confirmCo.description: Primary confirm button in the transfer dialog when the operation is copy. Control label.

@fileOperations.transferDialog.confirmMo.description: Primary confirm button in the transfer dialog when the operation is move. Control label.

@fileOperations.scanPhase.fromLabel.description: Label before the source path shown during the scan phase of a transfer, e.g. 'From: /Users/...'. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.scanPhase.scanFile.description: Unit noun after the live file tally during a transfer's scan phase (e.g. '123 files'). The number is rendered separately.

placeholders.count: the file count (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.scanPhase.scanDir.description: Unit noun after the live directory tally during a transfer's scan phase (e.g. '4 dirs'). 'dir' abbreviates directory/folder. The number is rendered separately.

placeholders.count: the directory count (drives the singular/plural noun)

@fileOperations.scanPhase.throughputFile.description: Live scan throughput during a transfer: files processed per second. '/s' means per second.

placeholders.rateText: the files-per-second rate, formatted for display (e.g. 1,200)

@fileOperations.transferProgress.scanTit.description: Progress-dialog title while counting files before a copy, so the user knows why the app is walking the folder tree. Keep the trailing ellipsis.
Progress-dialog title while counting files before a move. Keep the trailing ellipsis.
Progress-dialog title while counting items before a permanent delete. Keep the trailing ellipsis.
Progress-dialog title while counting items before moving them to trash. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

fileOperations.transferProgress.rollback: Rollback is not available for same-volume moves

@fileOperations.transferProgress.rollbac.description: Tooltip on the disabled Rollback button. A move within one disk/device renames in place and can't be undone step by step. 'Rollback' means undoing the operation so far.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titleRo.description: Progress-dialog title while undoing a cancelled copy/move (deleting the partial files it created). Keep the trailing ellipsis.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titleCa.description: Progress-dialog title right after the user cancels, while the operation stops. Keep the trailing ellipsis.
Progress-dialog title shown when cancellation takes a moment because an in-flight USB transfer must finish first. Keep the trailing ellipsis on the first part. 'USB' is the connection type.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titleCo.description: Progress-dialog title when a name clash needs the user to decide what to do. Deliberately plain; the destructive detail is spelled out in the body.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titleFl.description: Progress-dialog title during the final flush to disk after the bytes are copied (a real pause on slow media). Reassures the user it isn't stuck. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

fileOperations.transferProgress.titleAct: {gerund, select, copy {Copying} move {Moving} delete {Deleting} other {Moving to trash}}...

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titleAc.description: Progress-dialog title during the operation itself, in the -ing form of the verb. 'Moving to trash' is the trash variant. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

placeholders.gerund: the operation: copy, move, delete, or trash

@fileOperations.transferProgress.stageSc.description: Label of the first step chip in the progress dialog's two-step indicator: counting files. Short status label.

fileOperations.transferProgress.stageAct: {gerund, select, copy {Copying} move {Moving} delete {Deleting} other {Moving to trash}}

@fileOperations.transferProgress.stageAc.description: Label of the second step chip in the progress dialog's two-step indicator: the operation in progress, in the -ing form. No trailing ellipsis (this is a step chip, not the title).

placeholders.gerund: the operation: copy, move, delete, or trash

fileOperations.transferProgress.warningF: The target exists and is a folder. You''re about to overwrite it with a file by the same name. All contents of the target folder would be deleted and replaced by the file. What to do?

@fileOperations.transferProgress.warning.description: Red warning shown in the conflict step when a file would replace a same-named folder, deleting the folder's contents. <strong> wraps the emphasized words 'folder' and 'file'. Keep it stark; this is a destructive action.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.existin.description: Row label in the conflict comparison for the item already at the destination, when it is a folder. Keep the trailing colon.
Row label in the conflict comparison for the item already at the destination, when it is a file (and the incoming item is a folder). Keep the trailing colon.
Row label in the conflict comparison for the item already at the destination, when both items are the same type. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.newFold.description: Row label in the conflict comparison for the incoming item, when it is a folder. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.newFile.description: Row label in the conflict comparison for the incoming item, when it is a file (and the existing item is a folder). Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.newLabe.description: Row label in the conflict comparison for the incoming item, when both items are the same type. Keep the trailing colon.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.sizeUnk.description: Placeholder shown in the conflict comparison where a size would go but the app couldn't determine it (e.g. a folder's size with no index). Keep the parentheses.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.annotat.description: Annotation next to whichever item in the conflict comparison is bigger. Keep the parentheses; it sits inline after a size.
Annotation next to whichever item in the conflict comparison was modified more recently. Keep the parentheses; it sits inline after a date.

fileOperations.transferProgress.smallerD: Can''t compare: target folder size is unknown.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.smaller.description: Tooltip on the disabled 'Overwrite all smaller' button when the destination is a folder whose size is unknown, so a size comparison isn't possible. The dash is an en dash.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.conflic.description: Conflict button: leave this one clashing item untouched. Short control label.
Conflict button: leave every remaining clashing item untouched. Short control label.
Conflict button: keep both by giving this incoming item a new name. Short control label.
Conflict button: keep both for every remaining clash by renaming the incoming items. Short control label.
Conflict button: replace this one existing item with the incoming one. Short control label.
Conflict button: replace every remaining clashing item with the incoming ones. Short control label.
Conflict button for a file-over-folder clash: replace this existing folder (and its contents) with the incoming file. Destructive; the label spells out the swap.
Conflict button for file-over-folder clashes: replace every clashing folder with the incoming file of the same name. Destructive; the label spells out the swap.
Conflict button: replace existing items only where the existing one is smaller than the incoming one. Control label.
Conflict button: replace existing items only where the existing one is older than the incoming one. Control label.
Button in the conflict step that stops the whole operation. Short control label.
Button in the conflict step that stops and undoes the operation so far (deletes the partial files it created). 'Rollback' means undoing the work done so far.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.progres.description: Label for the byte-progress bar in the progress dialog (how many bytes done out of total). Short label.
Label for the count-progress bar during a trash operation, where the unit is mixed items. Short label.
Label for the count-progress bar during copy/move/delete, where the unit is files. Short label.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.sizePro.description: Accessibility label for the byte-progress bar (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.transferProgress.filePro.description: Accessibility label for the count-progress bar (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.transferProgress.etaRema.description: Estimated time left, shown next to the progress bar, e.g. '~2m 30s remaining'. The ~ marks it as an estimate; the duration is already formatted.

placeholders.duration: the estimated time left, already formatted (e.g. 2m 30s)

fileOperations.transferProgress.smbNativ: This share uses the system connection. Cancel and rollback may be delayed.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.smbNati.description: Note during a transfer to an operating-system-mounted SMB share, warning that cancel and rollback may lag. 'Rollback' means undoing the work so far.

fileOperations.transferProgress.rollback: Cancel and delete any partial target files created

@fileOperations.transferProgress.rollbac.description: Tooltip on the Rollback button explaining it stops the operation and removes the half-written files it created so far.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.titlePa.description: Title of the progress dialog while the transfer is paused. The user paused it and can resume it; nothing is being copied right now. Short status word.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.pause.description: Button on the progress dialog that pauses this transfer in place. It can be resumed later. Toggles to "Resume". Short control label.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.resume.description: Button on the progress dialog, shown while the transfer is paused, that resumes it. Toggles back from "Pause". Short control label.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.pauseAr.description: Screen-reader label for the Pause button on the progress dialog (the visible button shows only "Pause").

@fileOperations.transferProgress.resumeA.description: Screen-reader label for the Resume button on the progress dialog (the visible button shows only "Resume").

@fileOperations.transferProgress.queue.description: Button on the progress dialog that sends this transfer to the background and opens the transfer queue window, so the user can keep working while it runs. It does not pause the transfer. Mirrors Total Commander's copy-dialog F2. Short control label.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.queueAr.description: Screen-reader label for the Queue button on the progress dialog (the visible button shows only "Queue"). Explains that the transfer moves to the background and is managed in the queue window.

fileOperations.transferProgress.queueToo: Keep this running in the background and manage it in the transfer queue (F2)

@fileOperations.transferProgress.queueTo.description: Tooltip on the Queue button explaining that the transfer keeps running in the background and is managed in the transfer queue window. F2 is the keyboard shortcut for the same action while this dialog is open.

fileOperations.transferProgress.queuedTo: {countText} ahead of this one, so it''s waiting its turn. Find it in the transfer queue.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.queuedT.description: Quiet toast shown when a new copy/move/delete can''t start right away because another transfer is busy on the same drive, so it waits in the queue. The queue window comes forward at the same time.

placeholders.countText: preformatted count phrase of transfers ahead, for example "1 transfer" or "3 transfers"

@fileOperations.transferProgress.queuedT.description: Count phrase of how many transfers are ahead of a newly-queued one, slotted into the queued toast (for example "1 transfer" or "3 transfers").

placeholders.count: how many transfers are ahead in the queue

fileOperations.transferProgress.backgrou: Still running in the background. Find it in the transfer queue.

@fileOperations.transferProgress.backgro.description: Quiet toast shown after the user sends a running transfer to the background (via the Queue button or F2). The transfer keeps going; the queue window comes forward to manage it.

@fileOperations.errorDialog.technicalDet.description: Toggle that expands raw, developer-facing details (paths, error codes) below a friendly transfer-error message. Control label.
Accessibility label for the read-only text box holding the raw technical error details (screen-reader only).

@fileOperations.errorDialog.retry.description: Button that re-attempts a failed copy/move/delete. Shown for errors worth trying again. Short control label.

@fileOperations.errorDialog.close.description: Button that dismisses the transfer-error dialog. Short control label.

@fileOperations.errorDialog.tooLargeAndM.description: Trailing line under the list of files that are too large for the destination drive, when more files are over the limit than the list shows. For example "and 3 more files".

placeholders.countText: the preformatted count of additional over-limit files not shown in the list (e.g. "3")
placeholders.count: the same count as a plain integer, used only to choose file/files

fileOperations.validation.disallowedChar: {kind, select, folder {Folder name can''t contain "/" or null characters} other {Filename can''t contain "/" or null characters}}

@fileOperations.validation.disallowedCha.description: Inline validation message shown live as the user types a name in the rename / new-file / new-folder field, when the name includes a slash or a null character (both forbidden on the file system). The "/" is a literal slash character and must stay as-is. Write each branch as a complete, natural sentence for a file versus a folder (the noun and its agreement differ across languages).

placeholders.kind: what is being named: "folder" for a folder, anything else for a file

fileOperations.validation.empty: {kind, select, folder {Folder name can''t be empty} other {Filename can''t be empty}}

@fileOperations.validation.empty.description: Inline validation message shown live as the user types a name in the rename / new-file / new-folder field, when the name is blank (or only whitespace). Write each branch as a complete, natural sentence for a file versus a folder.

placeholders.kind: what is being named: "folder" for a folder, anything else for a file

fileOperations.validation.nameTooLong: {kind, select, folder {Folder name is too long ({byteCount}/{maxBytes} bytes)} other {Filename is too long ({byteCount}/{maxBytes} bytes)}}

@fileOperations.validation.nameTooLong.description: Inline validation message shown live as the user types a name in the rename / new-file / new-folder field, when the name exceeds the file system''s per-name byte limit. "bytes" is the unit (a name can use more than one byte per character). Write each branch as a complete, natural sentence for a file versus a folder.

placeholders.kind: what is being named: "folder" for a folder, anything else for a file
placeholders.byteCount: how many bytes the typed name currently uses (e.g. 260)
placeholders.maxBytes: the maximum bytes allowed for one name (e.g. 255)

fileOperations.validation.pathTooLong: Full path is too long ({byteCount}/{maxBytes} bytes)

@fileOperations.validation.pathTooLong.description: Inline validation message shown live as the user types a name in the rename / new-file / new-folder field, when the resulting full path (parent folder plus the name) exceeds the file system''s total path byte limit. "bytes" is the unit. "Full path" means the entire path, parent folders included.

placeholders.byteCount: how many bytes the full resulting path currently uses (e.g. 1100)
placeholders.maxBytes: the maximum bytes allowed for a full path (e.g. 1024)

fileOperations.validation.extensionChang: Changing the file extension isn''t allowed (was "{oldExt}")

@fileOperations.validation.extensionChan.description: Inline validation message shown live while renaming a file, when the user changed the file extension but their setting forbids extension changes. "{oldExt}" is the original extension WITH its leading dot (e.g. ".txt"); it''s the user''s own filename text, so keep it verbatim and structure the sentence to tolerate any extension.

placeholders.oldExt: the original file extension including its leading dot, e.g. ".txt" (an uncontrolled value taken from the user''s filename)

@fileOperations.validation.conflict.description: Inline validation WARNING (not a hard error) shown live while renaming or creating, when a file or folder with the same name already exists in the target folder (matching is case-insensitive). "{name}" is the name the user typed; it''s uncontrolled user text, so keep it verbatim and structure the sentence to tolerate any name, casing, or length.

placeholders.name: the name the user typed that collides with an existing item (an uncontrolled value, shown in quotes)

@fileOperations.validation.pathEmpty.description: Inline validation message for a directory-path field (e.g. the transfer destination or go-to-path), shown when the path is blank or only whitespace. "Path" means a full file-system path.

@fileOperations.validation.pathNotAbsolu.description: Inline validation message for a directory-path field, shown when the path doesn''t start at the file-system root. "absolute" means a full path from the root; the "/" is the literal root slash and must stay as-is.

@fileOperations.validation.pathNullChar.description: Inline validation message for a directory-path field, shown when the path contains a null character (forbidden in file-system paths). "null character" is the technical name for the zero byte.

@fileOperations.validation.pathTotalTooL.description: Inline validation message for a directory-path field, shown when the whole path exceeds the file system''s total byte limit. "bytes" is the unit.

placeholders.byteCount: how many bytes the path currently uses (e.g. 1100)
placeholders.maxBytes: the maximum bytes allowed for a full path (e.g. 1024)

fileOperations.validation.pathSegmentToo: A folder name in the path is too long ({byteCount}/{maxBytes} bytes)

@fileOperations.validation.pathSegmentTo.description: Inline validation message for a directory-path field, shown when one folder name inside the path exceeds the per-name byte limit (even though the whole path fits). "bytes" is the unit.

placeholders.byteCount: how many bytes the offending folder name uses (e.g. 260)
placeholders.maxBytes: the maximum bytes allowed for one name (e.g. 255)
